channel.title: MarketWatch.com - Top Stories
channel.description: MarketWatch, a leading publisher of business and financial news, offers users up-to-the minute news, investment tools, and subscription products.
channel.copyright: Copyright © Dow Jones & Company, Inc. All rights reserved.

item.title: Did Trump just pull a ‘TACO’ on Iran? Why markets will remain volatile, even if investors see some relief from selling this week.
item.description: The Dow is off its session highs Monday after Tehran pushed back against President Trump’s claim of a potential de-escalation of the Iran conflict.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Adinolfi
Christine Idzelis

item.title: ‘Do you believe in Elon?’: Musk tests Tesla investors’ faith with an expensive chip-making plan
item.description: The Terafab, a planned venture between SpaceX and Tesla, could cost trillions of dollars, according to analysts.
item.dc:creator: William Gavin

item.title: Global oil prices fall to $100 a barrel after Trump delays strikes on Iranian power plants. Why further declines may be limited.
item.description: Oil prices fell sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said the U.S. and Iran had “very good and productive conversations” in the past two days.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Wang
Myra P. Saefong

item.title: Super Micro’s stock rises, but an analyst warns that ‘flattish’ growth may lie ahead after co-founder’s indictment
item.description: An analyst just downgraded Super Micro’s stock on concerns that the export-control scandal involving the server company’s co-founder will weigh on customer trust.
item.dc:creator: Christine Ji

item.title: Apple’s stock rises as enthusiasm builds for a potential foldable iPhone
item.description: Analysts point to strong consumer interest in what could be a “major form-factor change” for Apple.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Pedone

item.title: Soaring healthcare costs are forcing people to make troubling trade-offs, like skipping meals — and the situation is about to get worse
item.description: Here’s one woman’s plan: “Shopping for cheaper groceries, not buying clothes, avoiding getting sick, not being as social.”
item.dc:creator: Andrew Keshner

item.title: Travel stocks are among the biggest gainers as Trump teases Iran talks
item.description: Travel-related stocks such as airlines and cruise companies rallied Monday after President Donald Trump postponed his deadline for more strikes in Iran, saying the two countries had held “very good and productive” talks — something Iranian media reports rebuffed.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Assis

item.title: Fed’s interest-rate stance is shifting, and Goolsbee’s comments may be the latest evidence
item.description: The monetary-policy outlook at the Federal Reserve appears to be shifting away from rate cuts and toward potential tightening.
item.dc:creator: Greg Robb

item.title: The same Polymarket trader who predicted the start of the Iran war is now betting on a cease-fire by next week
item.description: A group of 10 recently opened accounts has bet thousands of dollars on Polymarket’s “US x Iran cease-fire” market.
item.dc:creator: Gordon Gottsegen

item.title: I’m 76 and won $50,000 in a settlement related to cancer from nuclear waste. What should I do with it?
item.description: “The money is tax-free and does not affect our income, which comes from investments and Social Security.”
item.dc:creator: Quentin Fottrell
